title: Migraine symptoms and associated features
text: Migraine is a common neurological disorder characterized by recurrent headache attacks. These attacks are often moderate to severe and may be accompanied by nausea, vomiting, photophobia, and phonophobia. In many patients, physical activity worsens symptoms. Some people experience aura before headache onset. Differential diagnosis includes tension-type headache and secondary causes of headache. Clinical evaluation should consider red flag symptoms such as sudden thunderclap onset, focal neurological deficits, fever, confusion, head trauma, or progressive worsening over time. Management depends on attack severity, frequency, associated symptoms, and the patient’s medical history. Resting in a dark and quiet room may help some patients. Hydration and avoiding known triggers may also be useful. However, symptom overlap with other conditions means that migraine cannot be confirmed from a short description alone.
